{ Scene } from 'phaser';\\\\\\\\nimport { EventBridge } from '../EventBridge';\\\\\\\\nimport { useGameStore } from '@/store/gameStore';\\\\\\\\nimport type { MovementController, MovementContext, Direction } from '@/types/game';\\\\\\\\nimport type { IControllablePlayer } from '@/types/game';\\\\\\\\nimport { TILE_SIZE, PLAYER_MOVE_DURATION, PLAYER_MOVE_DURATION_SLOW } from '@/config/gameConfig';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nconst MOVEMENT_BLOCKED_PHASES = ['dialogue', 'reading'] as const;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nconst KEY_MAP: Record&lt;string, Direction&gt; = {\\\\\\\\n  ArrowUp: 'up',\\\\\\\\n  ArrowDown: 'down',\\\\\\\\n  ArrowLeft: 'left',\\\\\\\\n  ArrowRight: 'right',\\\\\\\\n  KeyW: 'up',\\\\\\\\n  KeyS: 'down',\\\\\\\\n  KeyA: 'left',\\\\\\\\n  KeyD: 'right',\\\\\\\\n};\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Grid-based movement controller.\\\\\\\\n * Listens for arrow keys / WASD, checks collision, tweens sprite.\\\\\\\\n * Implements MovementController for swappability with FreeMovementController.\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport class GridMovement implements MovementController {\\\\\\\\n  private scene: Scene | null = null;\\\\\\\\n  private player: IControllablePlayer | null = null;\\\\\\\\n  private context: MovementContext | null = null;\\\\\\\\n  private isMoving = false;\\\\\\\\n  private boundHandler: ((e: KeyboardEvent) =&gt; void) | null = null;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  attach(\\\\\\\\n    scene: Scene,\\\\\\\\n    player: IControllablePlayer,\\\\\\\\n    context: MovementContext\\\\\\\\n  ): void {\\\\\\\\n    this.scene = scene;\\\\\\\\n    this.player = player;\\\\\\\\n    this.context = context;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    this.boundHandler = this.handleKeyDown.bind(this);\\\\\\\\n    scene.input.keyboard!.on('keydown', this.boundHandler);\\\\\\\\n    scene.events.once('shutdown', () =&gt; this.detach());\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  detach(): void {\\\\\\\\n    if (this.scene?.input?.keyboard &amp;&amp; this.boundHandler) {\\\\\\\\n      this.scene.input.keyboard.off('keydown', this.boundHandler);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    this.scene = null;\\\\\\\\n    this.player = null;\\\\\\\\n    this.context = null;\\\\\\\\n    this.boundHandler = null;\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  private handleKeyDown(event: KeyboardEvent): void {\\\\\\\\n    if (!this.scene || !this.player || !this.context || this.isMoving) return;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const gamePhase = useGameStore.getState().session.gamePhase;\\\\\\\\n    if (MOVEMENT_BLOCKED_PHASES.includes(gamePhase)) return;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const dir = KEY_MAP[event.code];\\\\\\\\n    if (!dir) return;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    event.preventDefault();\\\\\\\\n    this.tryMove(dir);\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  private tryMove(direction: Direction): boolean {\\\\\\\\n    if (!this.scene || !this.player || !this.context) return false;\\\\\\\\n    if (this.isMoving) return false;\\\\\\\\n    const gamePhase = useGameStore.getState().session.gamePhase;\\\\\\\\n    if (MOVEMENT_BLOCKED_PHASES.includes(gamePhase)) return false;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const pos = this.player.getGridPosition();\\\\\\\\n    const { dx, dy } = directionDelta(direction);\\\\\\\\n    const targetX = pos.x + dx;\\\\\\\\n    const targetY = pos.y + dy;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    if (!this.isValidTarget(targetX, targetY)) return false;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const isFlooded = this.context!.getFloodedTiles?.().has(&#96;${targetX},${targetY}&#96;);\\\\\\\\n    const duration = isFlooded ? PLAYER_MOVE_DURATION_SLOW : PLAYER_MOVE_DURATION;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    this.isMoving = true;\\\\\\\\n    EventBridge.emit('player-moving');\\\\\\\\n    this.player.setDirection(direction);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const pixelTargetX = targetX * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;\\\\\\\\n    const pixelTargetY = targetY * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    this.scene.tweens.add({\\\\\\\\n      targets: this.player.getSprite(),\\\\\\\\n      x: pixelTargetX,\\\\\\\\n      y: pixelTargetY,\\\\\\\\n      duration,\\\\\\\\n      ease: 'Linear',\\\\\\\\n      onComplete: () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        this.player!.setGridPosition(targetX, targetY);\\\\\\\\n        this.isMoving = false;\\\\\\\\n        EventBridge.emit('player-moved', { x: targetX, y: targetY });\\\\\\\\n      },\\\\\\\\n    });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    return true;\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  private isValidTarget(tx: number, ty: number): boolean {\\\\\\\\n    if (!this.context) return false;\\\\\\\\n    const { wallLayer, mapWidth, mapHeight, getBlockedTiles } = this.context;\\\\\\\\n    if (tx &lt; 0 || tx &gt;= mapWidth || ty &lt; 0 || ty &gt;= mapHeight) return false;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    if (getBlockedTiles?.().has(&#96;${tx},${ty}&#96;)) return false;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const tile = wallLayer.getTileAt(tx, ty);\\\\\\\\n    if (!tile || tile.index === -1) return true;\\\\\\\\n    const WALL_INDICES = [4, 5];\\\\\\\\n    return !WALL_INDICES.includes(tile.index);\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nfunction directionDelta(dir: Direction): { dx: number; dy: number } {\\\\\\\\n  switch (dir) {\\\\\\\\n    case 'up': return { dx: 0, dy: -1 };\\\\\\\\n    case 'down': return { dx: 0, dy: 1 };\\\\\\\\n    case 'left': return { dx: -1, dy: 0 };\\\\\\\\n    case 'right': return { dx: 1, dy: 0 };\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":131}\\\",\\\"toolCallBinary\\\":\\\"\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":15}\",\"42eb3f9c-ec79-4711-9d79-2d004b3f1a3b\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"716282d3-9be0-4dc5-9a94-0fb2e1241dec\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-02-28T11:40:34.449Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"tool_a096aef0-792a-40de-9edb-5ab85c69711\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"tool_a096aef0-792a-40de-9edb-5ab85c69711\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/sethwilson/dev/starship-alexandria/src/game/systems/Interaction.ts\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/sethwilson/dev/starship-alexandria/src/game/systems/Interaction.ts\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/sethwilson/dev/starship-alexandria/src/game/systems/Interaction.ts\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"import { Scene } from 'phaser';\\\\\\\\nimport { EventBridge } from '../EventBridge';\\\\\\\\nimport { useGameStore } from '@/store/gameStore';\\\\\\\\nimport type { IControllablePlayer, Interactive } from '@/types/game';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nconst INTERACTION_BLOCKED_PHASES = ['dialogue', 'reading'] as const;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n// Global lock to prevent interaction processing during state transitions\\\\\\\\nlet interactionLocked = false;\\\\\\\\nexport function lockInteractions() { interactionLocked = true; }\\\\\\\\nexport function unlockInteractions() { interactionLocked = false; }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Proximity-based interaction detection.\\\\\\\\n * 'on' interactives (books, journals, transporter): prompt when standing ON the tile.\\\\\\\\n * 'adjacent' interactives (NPCs): prompt when standing next to the tile (solid, block movement).\\\\\\\\n * Emits interaction-available and interaction-triggered via EventBridge.\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport class InteractionSystem {\\\\\\\\n  private scene: Scene | null = null;\\\\\\\\n  private player: IControllablePlayer | null = null;\\\\\\\\n  private interactives: Interactive[] = [];\\\\\\\\n  private currentInteractive: Interactive | null = null;\\\\\\\\n  private boundKeyHandler: ((e: KeyboardEvent) =&gt; void) | null = null;\\\\\\\\n  private isPlayerMoving = false;\\\\\\\\n  private lastInteractionTime = 0;\\\\\\\\n  private onPlayerMoving = () =&gt; { this.isPlayerMoving = true; };\\\\\\\\n  private onPlayerMoved = () =&gt; { this.isPlayerMoving = false; };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  /** Register an interactive object (called when entities spawn) */\\\\\\\\n  register(interactive: Interactive): void {\\\\\\\\n    if (!this.interactives.some((i) =&gt; i.id === interactive.id)) {\\\\\\\\n      this.interactives.push(interactive);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  unregister(id: string): void {\\\\\\\\n    this.interactives = this.interactives.filter((i) =&gt; i.id !== id);\\\\\\\\n    if (this.currentInteractive?.id === id) {\\\\\\\\n      this.currentInteractive = null;\\\\\\\\n      EventBridge.emit('interaction-available', { type: '', label: undefined });\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  /** Attach to scene and player; call from ExploreScene.create() */\\\\\\\\n  attach(scene: Scene, player: IControllablePlayer): void {\\\\\\\\n    this.scene = scene;\\\\\\\\n    this.player = player;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    this.boundKeyHandler = (e: KeyboardEvent) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      if (e.repeat) return; // Ignore key repeats\\\\\\\\n      if (e.code === 'KeyE' || e.code === 'Space') {\\\\\\\\n        // Cooldown: ignore interactions within 300ms of last one\\\\\\\\n        const now = Date.now();\\\\\\\\n        if (now - this.lastInteractionTime &lt; 300) return;\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        const gamePhase = useGameStore.getState().session.gamePhase;\\\\\\\\n        if (INTERACTION_BLOCKED_PHASES.includes(gamePhase)) return;\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        // Auto-unlock if we're in exploring phase (safeguard against stuck lock)\\\\\\\\n        if (gamePhase === 'exploring') {\\\\\\\\n          interactionLocked = false;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        // Check global lock (only blocks during state transitions)\\\\\\\\n        if (interactionLocked) return;\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        const target = this.currentInteractive;\\\\\\\\n        if (!target) return;\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        this.lastInteractionTime = now;\\\\\\\\n        e.preventDefault();\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        // Lock interactions before emitting\\\\\\\\n        interactionLocked = true;\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        // Emit immediately - the cooldown and lock prevent double-triggering\\\\\\\\n        EventBridge.emit('interaction-triggered', {\\\\\\\\n          type: target.type,\\\\\\\\n          id: target.id,\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    scene.input.keyboard!.on('keydown', this.boundKeyHandler);\\\\\\\\n    EventBridge.on('player-moving', this.onPlayerMoving);\\\\\\\\n    EventBridge.on('player-moved', this.onPlayerMoved);\\\\\\\\n    scene.events.once('shutdown', () =&gt; this.detach());\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  detach(): void {\\\\\\\\n    EventBridge.off('player-moving', this.onPlayerMoving);\\\\\\\\n    EventBridge.off('player-moved', this.onPlayerMoved);\\\\\\\\n    if (this.scene?.input?.keyboard &amp;&amp; this.boundKeyHandler) {\\\\\\\\n      this.scene.input.keyboard.off('keydown', this.boundKeyHandler);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    this.scene = null;\\\\\\\\n    this.player = null;\\\\\\\\n    this.interactives = [];\\\\\\\\n    this.currentInteractive = null;\\\\\\\\n    this.boundKeyHandler = null;\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  /** Call every frame; only shows prompt when standing on the interactive tile */\\\\\\\\n  update(): void {\\\\\\\\n    if (!this.player) return;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    if (this.isPlayerMoving) {\\\\\\\\n      if (this.currentInteractive) {\\\\\\\\n        this.currentInteractive = null;\\\\\\\\n        EventBridge.emit('interaction-available', { type: '', label: undefined });\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const pos = this.player.getGridPosition();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    const found = this.interactives.find((i) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      const range = i.interactionRange ?? 'on';\\\\\\\\n      if (range === 'on') {\\\\\\\\n        return i.gridX === pos.x &amp;&amp; i.gridY === pos.y;\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n      // adjacent: manhattan distance 1 (standing next to NPC)\\\\\\\\n      const dx = Math.abs(pos.x - i.gridX);\\\\\\\\n      const dy = Math.abs(pos.y - i.gridY);\\\\\\\\n      return (dx === 1 &amp;&amp; dy === 0) || (dx === 0 &amp;&amp; dy === 1);\\\\\\\\n    });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    if (found &amp;&amp; found.id !== this.currentInteractive?.id) {\\\\\\\\n      this.currentInteractive = found;\\\\\\\\n      EventBridge.emit('interaction-available', {\\\\\\\\n        type: found.type,\\\\\\\\n        label: found.label,\\\\\\\\n      });\\\\\\\\n    } else if (!found &amp;&amp; this.currentInteractive) {\\\\\\\\n      this.currentInteractive = null;\\\\\\\\n      EventBridge.emit('interaction-available', { type: '', label: undefined });\\\\\\\\n    } else if (found) {\\\\\\\\n      this.currentInteractive = found;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":142}\\\",\\\"toolCallBinary\\\":\\\"\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":15}\",\"fe052464-d1db-41c3-9ceb-59112cfdf9d8\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"470e0830-789e-473f-90d8-1f9d0fa1ffdf\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-02-28T11:40:34.449Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"thinking\\\":{\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"signature\\\":\\\"\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":30}\",\"9577d590-ce8a-461b-acd5-19b12c11f430\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"77f5f087-1a95-418a-859e-966ee1415745\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-02-28T11:40:34.449Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\\n## 1. Inventory Storage in gameStore\\\\n\\\\n### Persistent inventory (localStorage)\\\\nLocated in &#96;src/store/gameStore.ts&#96;:\\\\n\\\\n- Library: &#96;library: BookFragment[]&#96; — collected book fragments\\\\n  - Stored in persisted state\\\\n  - Actions: &#96;collectFragment()&#96;, &#96;openLibraryBook()&#96;\\\\n  - Duplicate prevention: checks &#96;library.some((f) =&gt; f.id === fragment.id)&#96;\\\\n\\\\n- Player inventory: &#96;player.spareBatteries: number&#96; — count of spare batteries\\\\n  - Actions: &#96;addBattery()&#96;, &#96;useBattery()&#96;\\\\n  - &#96;useBattery()&#96; only works if battery ≤ 50% (prevents waste)\\\\n\\\\n- Flashlight: &#96;player.flashlightBattery: number&#96; (0–100)\\\\n  - Actions: &#96;decrementFlashlight()&#96;, &#96;restoreFlashlight()&#96;, &#96;setFlashlight()&#96;\\\\n\\\\n- Exploration progress: &#96;exploration&#96; object\\\\n  - &#96;discoveredNPCs: string[]&#96;\\\\n  - &#96;readJournals: string[]&#96;\\\\n  - &#96;visitedMaps: string[]&#96;\\\\n  - &#96;totalFragmentsFound: number&#96;\\\\n\\\\n### Session-only state (not persisted)\\\\n- &#96;session.currentDialogue: DialogueLine[] | null&#96;\\\\n- &#96;session.currentBookFragment: BookFragment | null&#96;\\\\n- &#96;session.isReadingBook: boolean&#96;\\\\n- &#96;session.gamePhase: 'exploring' | 'ship' | 'dialogue' | 'reading'&#96;\\\\n\\\\n## 2. Keyboard Handling Patterns\\\\n\\\\n### React components (GameContainer, DialogueBox, BookDetail, LibraryShelf)\\\\nPattern in &#96;src/components/GameContainer.tsx&#96; (lines 34-69, 72-133):\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\\\nuseEffect(() =&gt; {\\\\n  const handleKey = (e: KeyboardEvent) =&gt; {\\\\n    if (e.repeat) return; // CRITICAL: ignore key repeats\\\\n    if (e.code !== 'KeyB') return; // Check specific key\\\\n    \\\\n    const { gamePhase } = useGameStore.getState().session;\\\\n    if (gamePhase === 'dialogue' || gamePhase === 'reading') return; // Block during overlays\\\\n    \\\\n    e.preventDefault();\\\\n    // Handle action...\\\\n  };\\\\n  \\\\n  window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKey);\\\\n  return () =&gt; window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKey);\\\\n}, [dependencies]);\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nKey patterns:\\\\n- Always check &#96;e.repeat&#96; first\\\\n- Check &#96;gamePhase&#96; to block during overlays\\\\n- Use &#96;window.addEventListener&#96; (not Phaser keyboard)\\\\n- Cleanup in &#96;useEffect&#96; return\\\\n\\\\n### Phaser systems (GridMovement, InteractionSystem)\\\\nPattern in &#96;src/game/systems/GridMovement.ts&#96; (lines 42-44):\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\\\nthis.boundHandler = this.handleKeyDown.bind(this);\\\\nscene.input.keyboard!.on('keydown', this.boundHandler);\\\\nscene.events.once('shutdown', () =&gt; this.detach());\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nPattern in &#96;src/game/systems/Interaction.ts&#96; (lines 50-84):\\\\n- Uses Phaser's &#96;scene.input.keyboard.on('keydown')&#96;\\\\n- Checks &#96;e.repeat&#96;, &#96;gamePhase&#96;, and interaction lock\\\\n- 300ms cooldown between interactions\\\\n\\\\n## 3. UI Overlay Toggling Pattern\\\\n\\\\n### State-driven visibility\\\\nComponents check &#96;gamePhase&#96; or specific state:\\\\n\\\\n- DialogueBox (&#96;src/components/DialogueBox.tsx&#96;):\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\\\n  const currentDialogue = useGameStore((s) =&gt; s.session.currentDialogue);\\\\n  const isOpen = lines.length &gt; 0;\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n- BookDetail (&#96;src/components/BookDetail.tsx&#96;):\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\\\n  const gamePhase = useGameStore((s) =&gt; s.session.gamePhase);\\\\n  const isOpen = gamePhase === 'reading' &amp;&amp; currentBookFragment !== null;\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n- LibraryShelf (&#96;src/components/LibraryShelf.tsx&#96;):\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\\\n  const gamePhase = useGameStore((s) =&gt; s.session.gamePhase);\\\\n  const isVisible = gamePhase === 'ship';\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n### Keyboard controls for overlays\\\\nCommon pattern (DialogueBox lines 82-114, BookDetail lines 41-54):\\\\n\\\\n1. Space/Enter: advance or close\\\\n2. Escape: close/cancel\\\\n3. Cooldown: ignore closes within 300ms of opening\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\\\n   const openedAtRef = useRef&lt;number&gt;(0);\\\\n   if (Date.now() - openedAtRef.current &lt; 300) return;\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n### Game phase transitions\\\\nActions that change &#96;gamePhase&#96;:\\\\n- &#96;openDialogue()&#96; → &#96;gamePhase: 'dialogue'&#96;\\\\n- &#96;collectFragment()&#96; → &#96;gamePhase: 'reading'&#96;\\\\n- &#96;closeDialogue()&#96; → &#96;gamePhase: 'exploring'&#96; or &#96;'reading'&#96;\\\\n- &#96;closeBook()&#96; → &#96;gamePhase: 'exploring'&#96; or &#96;'ship'&#96;\\\\n- &#96;beamToShip()&#96; → &#96;gamePhase: 'ship'&#96;\\\\n- &#96;beamToSurface()&#96; → &#96;gamePhase: 'exploring'&#96;\\\\n\\\\n### Movement/interaction blocking\\\\nBoth systems check &#96;MOVEMENT_BLOCKED_PHASES&#96; / &#96;INTERACTION_BLOCKED_PHASES&#96;:\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\\\nconst MOVEMENT_BLOCKED_PHASES = ['dialogue', 'reading'] as const;\\\\nif (MOVEMENT_BLOCKED_PHASES.includes(gamePhase)) return;\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n## Summary of Key Patterns\\\\n\\\\n1. Inventory: &#96;library&#96; array + &#96;spareBatteries&#96; count, persisted via Zustand persist middleware\\\\n2. Keyboard: always check &#96;e.repeat&#96;, check &#96;gamePhase&#96;, React uses &#96;window.addEventListener&#96;, Phaser uses &#96;scene.input.keyboard&#96;\\\\n3. Overlays: controlled by &#96;gamePhase&#96; state, Space/Enter/Escape handlers, 300ms cooldown on open\\\\n\\\\nAll keyboard handlers follow the &#96;e.repeat&#96; check pattern to prevent duplicate triggers from held keys.\\\"}\"},\"status\":\"completed\",\"context\":{\"composers\":[],\"selectedCommits\":[],\"selectedPullRequests\":[],\"selectedImages\":[],\"folderSelections\":[],\"fileSelections\":[],\"selections\":[],\"terminalSelections\":[],\"selectedDocs\":[],\"externalLinks\":[],\"cursorRules\":[],\"cursorCommands\":[],\"gitPRDiffSelections\":[],\"subagentSelections\":[],\"browserSelections\":[],\"mentions\":{\"composers\":{},\"selectedCommits\":{},\"selectedPullRequests\":{},\"gitDiff\":[],\"gitDiffFromBranchToMain\":[],\"selectedImages\":{},\"folderSelections\":{},\"fileSelections\":{},\"terminalFiles\":{},\"selections\":{},\"terminalSelections\":{},\"selectedDocs\":{},\"externalLinks\":{},\"diffHistory\":[],\"cursorRules\":{},\"cursorCommands\":{},\"uiElementSelections\":[],\"consoleLogs\":[],\"ideEditorsState\":[],\"gitPRDiffSelections\":{},\"subagentSelections\":{},\"browserSelections\":{}}},\"generatingBubbleIds\":[],\"isReadingLongFile\":false,\"codeBlockData\":{},\"originalFileStates\":{},\"newlyCreatedFiles\":[],\"newlyCreatedFolders\":[],\"createdAt\":1772275856038,\"hasChangedContext\":false,\"activeTabsShouldBeReactive\":true,\"capabilities\":[{\"type\":30,\"data\":{}},{\"type\":15,\"data\":{\"bubbleDataMap\":\"{}\"}},{\"type\":29,\"data\":{}}],\"name\":\"Game <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I now have a good understanding of the codebase patterns. Let me summarize what I learned:

1. **Interactables Pattern**:
   - Visual: Shadow → Outline → Colored Ring → Sprite → Pulse animation
   - Placed on reachable tiles, with adjacency checks
   - Registered with InteractionSystem
   - Events: `interaction-available` → `interaction-triggered` → `interactive-consumed`
   - Container at depth 5

2. **Map Data**:
   - `GeneratedMap.rooms[]` has room boundaries (x1, y1, x2, y2, centerX, centerY, name)
   - `walls[y][x]` indicates if tile is walkable (TILE.EMPTY) or wall
   - `getRoomAt()` utility function available
   - Can build ASCII representation from walls array

3. **Dialogue/TTS**:
   - Call `openDialogue([{ text: 'message' }])` 
   - DialogueBox automatically speaks via TTS
   - No need to manually call speak()

4. **Keyboard Handling**:
   - React: `window.addEventListener('keydown')` with `if (e.repeat) return`
   - Check `gamePhase` to block during overlays
   - Keys like B already handled in GameContainer

5. **Inventory**:
   - `player.spareBatteries` is example of simple item count
   - Could add `player.hasMap: boolean` or similar

Now let me think about clarifying questions:

1. **Map scope**: Should the map be per-area (resets when beaming to new surface) or carry over? Given "You haven't found the map to this area yet" - it sounds per-area.

2. **Where is the map placed?**: One map per generated map, or could there be multiple? Based on the TTS message, it seems like one map per area makes sense.


3. **Display format**: The map should show as a modal overlay with the game paused, similar to how the book detail view works. Room names need to be visible in the ASCII representation so the player can identify which rooms are where. Corridors should probably be shown but distinguished from rooms, since the user specifically asked about rooms. The core requirement is displaying rooms and their locations without showing item positions.

4. **Legend or labels**: I need to decide whether room names appear as labels directly on the map or in a separate legend. Given the user is legally blind and using a screen reader, clarity is essential here.

5. **Scope per area**: The map should reset when exploring a new surface area, with one map pickup per generated area. I should formulate a clear implementation plan with minimal clarifying questions, since the user seems to have a specific vision for this feature.</details></think>

```json
  "title": "Map Accessibility Clarification",

      "id": "tts_map",
      "prompt": "Given the accessibility focus, should the ASCII map have a TTS-friendly mode? For example, when viewing the map, pressing a key could read out room names and their relative positions (e.g., 'Library Wing in the northeast, Reading Room in the center...').",
      "options": [
        {
          "id": "visual_only",
          "label": "Visual ASCII only - no TTS navigation needed"
        },
        {
          "id": "basic_tts",
          "label": "Basic TTS - read room list when map opens"
        },
        {
          "id": "navigate_tts",
          "label": "Full TTS navigation - arrow keys move between rooms with spoken descriptions"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

The user wants full TTS navigation - arrow keys to move between rooms with spoken descriptions. This is a significant feature that will require:

1. A map overlay component that:
   - Shows ASCII representation of the map
   - Has a "cursor" that can move between rooms   - Speaks room name and position when navigating   - Handles arrow keys for navigation2. The map pickup item (visual + placement + interaction)

3. Store state for:
   - `hasAreaMap: boolean` - whether player has found the map for current area
   - Possibly `selectedRoomIndex: number` for the TTS navigation

4. Event handling for M key

Let me write a comprehensive plan.</details></think>

Parameters:

```json
{
  "plan": "# Add Map Pickup Item with TTS Navigation\n\n## Overview\n\nAdd a new interactable \"map\" object that follows existing visual patterns (glowing ring, pulse animation). When picked up, pressing M opens an ASCII map overlay showing room layout. The map includes full TTS navigation: arrow keys move between rooms, speaking room names and relative positions.\n\n## Key Files to Modify\n\n- [&#96;src/types/game.ts&#96;](src/types/game.ts) - Add &#96;'map'&#96; to &#96;InteractiveType&#96;\n- [&#96;src/store/gameStore.ts&#96;](src/store/gameStore.ts) - Add &#96;hasAreaMap&#96; to session state + actions\n- [&#96;src/game/scenes/BootScene.ts&#96;](src/game/scenes/BootScene.ts) - Create procedural map sprite\n- [&#96;src/game/scenes/ExploreScene.ts&#96;](src/game/scenes/ExploreScene.ts) - Place map pickup, handle consumption\n- [&#96;src/components/GameContainer.tsx&#96;](src/components/GameContainer.tsx) - Handle M key and interaction\n- [&#96;src/components/MapOverlay.tsx&#96;](src/components/MapOverlay.tsx) - New component for ASCII map display\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### 1. Type and Store Updates\n\nAdd &#96;'map'&#96; to &#96;InteractiveType&#96; union. Add session state:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nsession: {\n  hasAreaMap: boolean;      // Reset when beaming to new surface\n  mapRooms: GeneratedRoom[]; // Cached room data for current area\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nActions: &#96;collectMap(rooms: GeneratedRoom[])&#96;, &#96;clearAreaMap()&#96; (called on beam-to-surface)\n\n### 2. Map Sprite (BootScene)\n\nCreate procedural texture &#96;'map-pickup'&#96;:\n- Rolled/folded parchment look (tan/beige rectangle with darker edges)\n- Size: 24x24 to fit within highlight ring\n- Color scheme: &#96;0xf4e4bc&#96; (parchment) with &#96;0x8b7355&#96; (brown) accents\n\n### 3. Map Placement (ExploreScene)\n\nFollow existing interactable pattern:\n- One map per generated area\n- Placed in a random room (not spawn room, not adjacent to other interactables)\n- Visual: shadow, dark outline ring, cyan/teal highlight ring (&#96;0x00ced1&#96;), sprite, pulse\n- Container depth: 5\n- Register with &#96;InteractionSystem&#96; (type: &#96;'map'&#96;, range: &#96;'on'&#96;)\n\n### 4. Pickup Handling (GameContainer)\n\nOn &#96;interaction-triggered&#96; with type &#96;'map'&#96;:\n1. Call &#96;collectMap(mapData.rooms)&#96;\n2. Open dialogue: &#96;[{ text: \"You picked up the map.\" }]&#96;\n3. Emit &#96;interactive-consumed&#96;\n\n### 5. M Key Handler (GameContainer)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nif (e.code === 'KeyM') {\n  if (!hasAreaMap) {\n    openDialogue([{ text: \"You haven't found the map to this area yet.\" }]);\n  } else {\n    setGamePhase('viewing-map'); // New phase\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 6. MapOverlay Component\n\nNew React component that renders when &#96;gamePhase === 'viewing-map'&#96;:\n\n**ASCII Display:**\n- Monospace font, dark background\n- Characters: &#96;#&#96; walls, &#96;.&#96; room floors, &#96;+&#96; corridors, &#96;@&#96; player position\n- Room names displayed in legend below map\n\n**TTS Navigation:**\n- On open: speak \"Map of [area]. [N] rooms. Use arrow keys to navigate rooms. Press Escape to close.\"\n- Arrow keys cycle through rooms (sorted by position: top-to-bottom, left-to-right)\n- On navigation: speak \"[Room Name]. [Direction] side of the map.\" (e.g., \"Library Wing. Northeast.\")\n- Escape or M closes overlay\n\n**Visual Accessibility:**\n- Current room highlighted with distinct color/border\n- High contrast: white text on near-black background\n- Large monospace font for readability\n\n### 7. Reset on New Exploration\n\nWhen &#96;beamToSurface()&#96; is called, also call &#96;clearAreaMap()&#96; to reset &#96;hasAreaMap: false&#96;. Each area requires finding its own map.\n\n## Data Flow\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nPlayer steps on map tile\n    ↓\nInteractionSystem emits 'interaction-available'\n    ↓\nPlayer presses E/Space\n    ↓\nInteractionSystem emits 'interaction-triggered' { type: 'map' }\n    ↓\nGameContainer calls collectMap(rooms), openDialogue(), emits 'interactive-consumed'\n    ↓\nExploreScene destroys map container, unregisters from InteractionSystem\n    ↓\nPlayer presses M\n    ↓\nGameContainer checks hasAreaMap, sets gamePhase: 'viewing-map'\n    ↓\nMapOverlay renders with TTS navigation\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n",
  "name": "Add Map Pickup Item",
  "todos": [

      "status": "pending",

  ],
  "overview": "Add a collectible map item that, when picked up, allows the player to view an ASCII representation of the current area's layout with full TTS navigation support (arrow keys to move between rooms with spoken descriptions)."
}
```

2. Add an M key handler that checks `hasAreaMap` and either opens the map or shows "You haven't found the map to this area yet" dialogueLet me also check how the mapData (rooms, walls, spawn) would be passed to the store. I need to pass the map data when the player picks up the map.

Looking at ExploreScene, I see the mapData is available there. But the GameContainer doesn't have direct access to it. I need to think about this.

Options:1. Store the map data in Zustand session when the map is generated (at ExploreScene create)
2. Emit the map data via EventBridge when the map is picked up

Option 2 seems cleaner - the map data only needs to be stored when the player picks up the map. But where would the map data come from in GameContainer?

Actually, looking more carefully - ExploreScene has access to `this.mapData` which includes rooms, walls, spawnX, spawnY. When the map is picked up, I could emit the data via EventBridge.

Let me approach it this way:
1. In ExploreScene, when placing the map, I'll add an event that passes the map data when the map interaction is triggered
2. Alternatively, I could store the map data in Zustand session at ExploreScene.create() time, and just flip hasAreaMap when the map is picked up

Actually, option 2 is simpler. Let me store the map layout data at scene creation time, and collecting the map just flips `hasAreaMap` to true.

Wait, but the plan says `collectMap(rooms, walls, spawn)` - so the data is passed when collecting. Let me follow the plan.

I'll make ExploreScene emit the map data when the map is picked up. But GameContainer handles `interaction-triggered` and doesn't have access to the ExploreScene's mapData.


The cleanest path forward is to populate the map data in Zustand during ExploreScene initialization, then simply toggle `hasAreaMap` when the pickup occurs. This sidesteps the need to thread data through EventBridge and keeps the collection action straightforward. I'll store the rooms, walls, and spawn coordinates at scene creation, then modify the approach to add the necessary store fields. The issue is that GameContainer needs access to the map data when handling the interaction, but it doesn't have it. I could emit a custom event from ExploreScene with the data included, or attach it to the interactive registration. The simplest approach is to store the map data directly in Zustand when ExploreScene initializes, so it's available whenever GameContainer needs to call `collectMap()`. (sets the data but hasAreaMap stays false)
2. `collectMap()` in store just sets `hasAreaMap: true` (data is already there)
3. `clearAreaMap()` clears everything including hasAreaMap

This way GameContainer just calls `collectMap()` with no arguments. I'll update the store to support this pattern.</details></think>

---

The build succeeded. All implementation is complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Summary of Changes

**1. Types and Store** (`src/types/game.ts`, `src/types/store.ts`, `src/store/gameStore.ts`)
- Added `'map'` to `InteractiveType`
- Added `MapRoom` interface for room data
- Added `'viewing-map'` to `GamePhase`
- Added session state: `hasAreaMap`, `mapRooms`, `mapWalls`, `mapSpawn`
- Added actions: `setMapLayoutData()`, `collectMap()`, `clearAreaMap()`, `openMap()`, `closeMap()`
- Modified `beamToSurface()` to reset map state when entering new areas

**2. Map Sprite** (`src/game/scenes/BootScene.ts`)
- Created procedural `'map-pickup'` texture: rolled parchment with grid lines and X mark

**3. Map Placement** (`src/game/scenes/ExploreScene.ts`)
- One map placed per area (not in spawn room, follows adjacency rules)
- Cyan/teal highlight ring (`0x00ced1`) with pulse animation
- Stores map layout data at scene creation
- Handles map consumption on `interactive-consumed`

**4. Game Controls** (`src/components/GameContainer.tsx`)
- Handles `'map'` interaction → collects map, shows "You picked up the map." dialogue
- M key handler: opens map overlay if collected, or says "You haven't found the map to this area yet."

**5. MapOverlay Component** (`src/components/MapOverlay.tsx`)
- ASCII representation: `#` walls, `.` rooms, `+` corridors, `@` player, `*` selected room
- Room list with directional positions (northeast, center, etc.)
- Full TTS navigation: Arrow keys cycle through rooms with spoken descriptions
- Intro speech on open, Escape/M to close

**6. Movement/Interaction Blocking** (`src/game/systems/GridMovement.ts`, `src/game/systems/Interaction.ts`)
- Added `'viewing-map'` to blocked phases

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Looking at the screenshot, I can see the schematic view on the right side has room boxes stacked vertically in a list, but they're not positioned according to their actual map coordinates. The left side shows the actual game map with rooms in their proper positions.

The user is right - the schematic view should show rooms laid out spatially like they are on the actual map, not stacked in a vertical list. Looking at my code, I see the issue - I'm positioning the rooms correctly with `left` and `top` based on their coordinates, but looking at the screenshot, it seems like the rooms are all stacked.

Wait, looking more carefully at the screenshot - I see the schematic on the right side with colored boxes. They do appear to be positioned, but the room name labels are appearing separately stacked on the left side of the schematic area. Let me look at my code again...

Actually, I think I see the issue. The schematic boxes are there and positioned correctly, but they might be overlapping or the container isn't sized properly. Let me check the scale calculation.

Looking at the code:
```javascript
const scale = Math.min(500 / mapSpanX, 400 / mapSpanY, 12);
```

If mapSpanX and mapSpanY are around 50 (the full map size), then scale would be around 10. And the container width would be `50 * 10 + 32 = 532px`.

But the rooms themselves might be small or overlapping. Let me look at the room rendering:
```javascript
const left = (room.x1 - minX) * scale;
const top = (room.y1 - minY) * scale;
const width = (room.x2 - room.x1 + 1) * scale;
const height = (room.y2 - room.y1 + 1) * scale;
```

This looks correct. But looking at the screenshot more carefully, I see the schematic area is showing boxes, but there's also a separate "Rooms" list below that's just a text list. 

The colored boxes on the right are actually positioned spatially - I can see the cyan highlighted room and gray ones arranged properly. The issue is that the room labels inside those boxes are overflowing or positioned incorrectly, likely because the font size calculation isn't accounting for small room dimensions properly.

With the current formula, a small 5-tile room at scale 10 would only get 9px text, which is too cramped. The real problems are overlapping rooms and labels that are difficult to read in the confined spaces. I should increase the minimum scale to give rooms more breathing room, reposition labels more intelligently, or use hover tooltips instead of trying to force text into tiny boxes.

Looking at the actual layout, the schematic boxes are positioned correctly relative to the map, but the room names—scriptorium, courtyard, archives, library wing, stacks, vestibule, study, reading room—are crammed into the boxes or relegated to a side list. The spatial relationships are clear, but identifying individual rooms is frustrating. I'll switch to a numbered marker system with a legend instead, keeping the room list as the primary reference while the schematic shows how spaces relate to each other.</details></think>

Let me implement this:

- Fog of war / explored tiles in session state

The visual state (where sprites are, etc.) would need to be recreated, but actually... we could just pause the scene instead of stopping it. Phaser supports scene sleeping/waking.

Let me create a MapScene that:
1. Is started when M is pressed (and hasAreaMap is true)
2. ExploreScene is put to sleep (scene.sleep())
3. MapScene renders the map
4. On close, MapScene stops and ExploreScene wakes up (scene.wake())


This preserves all the visual state without needing to rebuild anything - the scene just pauses and resumes when the map closes.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
There's a dev server running. It looks like one of them may have just started (424750.txt running_for_seconds: 0). The game should be available at localhost:8080.

The changes I made:
1. Created a new `MapScene.ts` that renders rooms as a proper Phaser scene
2. Registered it in `main.ts`
3. Added an `open-map-scene` event to EventBridge
4. Updated GameContainer's M key handler to emit this event
5. Updated ExploreScene to listen for this event, sleep itself, and run MapScene
6. Removed the React MapOverlay from the render tree

Now the map should render as a clean Phaser scene with properly positioned rooms. The TTS navigation was already mentioned as working well, so that should still work since I ported that logic to the Phaser scene.

Let me make one small improvement - I should make sure the keyboard listener cleanup is handled properly when the scene is stopped. Let me review the MapScene code.

Actually, I just realized something - in the MapScene, I'm adding a keyboard listener via `this.input.keyboard!.on('keydown', ...)`. When the scene is stopped, I should clean that up. Let me check if Phaser handles this automatically when a scene is stopped, or if I need to remove it manually.

Actually, looking at my code in MapScene:
```ts
private closeMap() {
    cancelSpeech();
    this.input.keyboard!.off('keydown', this.handleKeyDown, this);
    useGameStore.getState().actions.closeMap();
    this.scene.stop('MapScene');
    this.scene.wake('ExploreScene');
  }
```

I am removing the listener before stopping the scene. This should be fine.

Let me also think about when ExploreScene wakes up. The GridMovement and InteractionSystem should resume working. Let me check if they have any issues with scene sleep/wake cycles.


The window event listeners in GridMovement and InteractionSystem will stay active while the scene sleeps, but that's okay since both systems check the gamePhase before processing input. With gamePhase set to 'viewing-map', they'll skip any movement or interaction logic. The gamePhase check acts as a guard that prevents these handlers from executing even though they're technically still listening.</details></think>

---

The implementation is complete. Here's a summary of what I changed:

**New file created:**
- `src/game/scenes/MapScene.ts` - A dedicated Phaser scene that:
  - Renders rooms as properly-scaled rectangles with rounded corners
  - Positions rooms spatially based on their actual map coordinates
  - Displays room names inside each box with dynamic font sizing
  - Shows the player's current location with a green border and "★ YOU" marker
  - Supports arrow key navigation with TTS for each room
  - Closes on Escape or M key

**Modified files:**
- `src/game/main.ts` - Added MapScene to the scene list
- `src/game/EventBridge.ts` - Added `open-map-scene` event type
- `src/game/scenes/ExploreScene.ts` - Added listener for `open-map-scene` that sleeps ExploreScene and runs MapScene
- `src/components/GameContainer.tsx` - Changed M key handler to emit `open-map-scene` event instead of using React overlay; removed MapOverlay import

**Key improvements:**
1. The map is now a full Phaser scene, completely separate from the game world
2. ExploreScene state is preserved via `scene.sleep()` and restored with `scene.wake()` 
3. No fighting between React overlay and Phaser rendering
4. Clean coordinate system - rooms are scaled to fit the screen with padding

Try pressing M with the map collected - you should now see a clean schematic in a dedicated scene!
